title: Show total number of steps when building images with bud
body:
Closes https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues/1681

When building images with `bud`, buildah now shows the total number of steps remaining, for example:
```
[root@080ea3d07748 microclimateGoTemplate]# buildah bud --storage-driver=vfs -t goproject .
STEP 1/8: FROM golang:latest
STEP 2/8: RUN mkdir /app 
STEP 3/8: ADD . /app/ 
STEP 4/8: WORKDIR /app 
STEP 5/8: RUN go build -o main . 
STEP 6/8: CMD ["/app/main"]
STEP 7/8: EXPOSE 8000
STEP 8/8: COMMIT goproject
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 0db06dff9d9a skipped: already exists
Copying blob f32868cde90b skipped: already exists
Copying blob 7b76d801397d skipped: already exists
Copying blob 2c8d31157b81 skipped: already exists
Copying blob 510e5f32af35 skipped: already exists
Copying blob 56df4f4c91ea skipped: already exists
Copying blob 39ba5a88a3c4 skipped: already exists
Copying blob c09a626e77bb done
Copying config ec4bc6fcb6 done
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
ec4bc6fcb62c6a0c3ea11dacda323841f6f4369d6c894fac0e394c08ae7533cc
```

I've also updated the tests under bud.bats to account for the change in the output now (STEP X -> STEP X/Y). Output from buds.bats:
```
[root@080ea3d07748 tests]# bats bud.bats 
 ✓ bud with --dns* flags
 ✓ bud with .dockerignore
 ✓ bud-flags-order-verification
 ✓ bud with --layers and --no-cache flags
 ✓ bud with --layers and single and two line Dockerfiles
 ✓ bud with --layers, multistage, and COPY with --from
 ✓ bud-multistage-copy-final-slash
 ✓ bud-multistage-reused
 ✓ bud-multistage-cache
 ✓ bud with --layers and symlink file
 ✓ bud with --layers and dangling symlink
 ✓ bud with --layers and --build-args
 ✓ bud with --rm flag
 ✓ bud with --force-rm flag
 ✓ bud --layers with non-existent/down registry
 ✓ bud from base image should have base image ENV also
 ✓ bud-from-scratch
 ✓ bud-from-scratch-iid
 ✓ bud-from-scratch-label
 ✓ bud-from-scratch-annotation
 ✓ bud-from-scratch-layers
 ✓ bud-from-multiple-files-one-from
 ✓ bud-from-multiple-files-two-froms
 ✓ bud-multi-stage-builds
 ✓ bud-multi-stage-builds-small-as
 - bud-preserve-subvolumes (skipped: no runc in PATH)
 ✓ bud-http-Dockerfile
 ✓ bud-http-context-with-Dockerfile
 ✓ bud-http-context-dir-with-Dockerfile-pre
 ✓ bud-http-context-dir-with-Dockerfile-post

 ✓ bud multi-stage with symlink to absolute path
 ✓ bud multi-stage with dir symlink to absolute path
 ✓ bud with ENTRYPOINT and RUN

 ✓ bud with ENTRYPOINT, CMD and RUN

117 tests, 0 failures, 3 skipped
```

Signed-off-by: John Collier <John.J.Collier@ibm.com>

comments.body:
Can one of the admins verify this patch?
I understand the following commands:
- `bot, add author to whitelist`
- `bot, test pull request`
- `bot, test pull request once`

Changes LGTM, but I want to run it through a couple tests.  @nalind WDYT?
I like the idea of it too!

I found one test in the baseline tests that's unfortunately not quite right.   Notice the '4/3' near the end.

```
########
# Build Dockerfiles for OnBuild Test
# (Thanks @clcollins!)
########
FILE=./Dockerfile
/bin/cat <<EOM >$FILE
FROM alpine
RUN touch /foo
ONBUILD RUN touch /bar
EOM
chmod +x $FILE

FILE=./Dockerfile-2
/bin/cat <<EOM >$FILE
FROM onbuild-image
RUN touch /baz
EOM
chmod +x $FILE

########
# Build with Dockerfiles 
########
buildah bud -f ./Dockerfile --format=docker -t onbuild-image .
STEP 1/4: FROM alpine
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob e7c96db7181b done
Copying config 055936d392 done
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
STEP 2/4: RUN touch /foo
STEP 3/4: ONBUILD RUN touch /bar
STEP 4/4: COMMIT onbuild-image
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob f1b5933fe4b5 skipped: already exists
Copying blob 22907af69a5d done
Copying config 41c3d3a577 done
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
41c3d3a577883629030d25b0f4064994a4646fe1c86f64a55fd94e160aedb1af
buildah bud -f ./Dockerfile-2 --format=docker -t result-image .
STEP 1/3: FROM onbuild-image
STEP 2/3: RUN touch /bar
STEP 3/3: RUN touch /baz
STEP 4/3: COMMIT result-image
```

I don't have a quick answer on how to adjust for this particular case.

@TomSweeneyRedHat Could it be an issue with using `mainNode.Children`? Is there an alternative way to get the total steps? My guess is that the `RUN touch /bar` (from the `ONBUILD RUN touch /bar` in the first Dockerfile) isn't getting included in the list of children?

@johnmcollier I was hoping to have some time to poke around today at this, but that thought got derailed.  I'm not sure there's already a variable that has a total set up within it currently.  I think the one you grabbed is probably the closest.  @nalind do you know of any off the top of your head that shows the total number of steps that will be completed at the time the first step is just starting?

@TomSweeneyRedHat The number of instructions (i.e., the number of nodes in the parsed tree) is a pretty good count - I think the logging helper functions are counting the final commit operation as a step (unless we're `--quiet`), so the expected count doesn't quite line up with the number of instructions, since there isn't an instruction being parsed there.
Can we bump the count by one unless --quiet is specified?
@johnmcollier @TomSweeneyRedHat Anything going on with this PR?

@rhatdan Sorry, been a little busy the past few days and haven't been able to revisit this.

When you say to bump the count by one, where do you mean doing that?
Do we have an off by 1 error here.  Where we always have N+1 steps to execute.  @nalind above explained why it was off by 1.

I didn't play, but I think there's a possibility that we might be off by more than +1 dependent on the Dockerfile and the number of images we're pulling in.  Let's say in the second Dockerfile in the above example if we had:
```
FROM onbuild-image
FROM onbuild-image2
```

Then we might be off by 2 rather than one.  But again, I didn't play.
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably 7fc4ca7) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.
@johnmcollier @vrothberg @TomSweeneyRedHat This PR and issue seems to have wilted on the vine.  Is there interest in re-opening this or should we just close it.
We'd run into problems with getting an accurate count in all instances.  Conceptually I love the idea, I'm just not sure if we can pull it off with accuracy.  I'd let it be for now.
I'm going to reopen, I've been thinking about this one a bit, probably a slow burn for a fix though.
@umohnani8 do you have some time to take a look at this?
A friendly reminder that this PR had no activity for 30 days.

@johnmcollier: PR needs rebase.

<details>

Instructions for interacting with me using PR comments are available [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/pull-requests.md).  If you have questions or suggestions related to my behavior, please file an issue against the [kubernetes/test-infra](https://github.com/kubernetes/test-infra/issues/new?title=Prow%20issue:) repository.
</details>
